parentQuestion: Positive feedback mechanisms tend to enhance the original stimulus so that the response is accelerated.

options: A control system where the output influences the input
scaffolds.explain: A FEEDBACK MECHANISM is a control system where the OUTPUT influences the INPUT. This allows the body to monitor conditions and respond appropriately.

wrongExplains.0: Feedback mechanisms are regulated, not random.
wrongExplains.2: Feedback mechanisms operate throughout the body, not just nervous system.
wrongExplains.3: Feedback mechanisms are found in all body systems, not just digestion.

scaffolds.q: What are the two main types of feedback?
scaffolds.explain: The two main types are NEGATIVE FEEDBACK (opposes changes) and POSITIVE FEEDBACK (amplifies changes). They serve opposite purposes.

wrongExplains.0: The classification is based on direction, not speed.
wrongExplains.2: The classification is based on effect, not location.
wrongExplains.3: The classification is based on effect, not mechanism type.

options: Opposes changes and brings things back toward normal
scaffolds.explain: NEGATIVE FEEDBACK OPPOSES changes — it brings things back toward normal. Like a thermostat: Too hot, cooling kicks in; too cold, heating kicks in.

wrongExplains.0: Amplification is positive feedback; negative feedback opposes.
wrongExplains.2: Negative feedback creates stability by opposing deviations.
wrongExplains.3: Negative feedback slows/reverses changes, not speeds them up.

options: Amplifies changes — makes the response bigger
scaffolds.explain: POSITIVE FEEDBACK AMPLIFIES changes — the output feeds back to INCREASE the original stimulus, making the response bigger and faster.

wrongExplains.0: Opposing changes is negative feedback; positive amplifies.
wrongExplains.2: Positive feedback drives toward completion, not stability.
wrongExplains.3: Positive feedback has powerful effects on body processes.

scaffolds.q: Which is an example of positive feedback?
options: Blood clotting — each step triggers MORE clotting
scaffolds.explain: BLOOD CLOTTING uses positive feedback — clotting factors activate MORE clotting factors in an explosive cascade until the wound is sealed.

wrongExplains.0: Temperature regulation is negative feedback; it opposes deviations.
wrongExplains.2: Blood pressure regulation is negative feedback.

scaffolds.q: How do childbirth contractions demonstrate positive feedback?
options: Contractions trigger stronger contractions until delivery
scaffolds.explain: During childbirth, OXYTOCIN causes contractions, which trigger MORE oxytocin, causing STRONGER contractions. The cycle continues until delivery.

wrongExplains.0: Contractions increase in positive feedback, not decrease.
wrongExplains.2: Positive feedback causes escalation, not constancy.
wrongExplains.3: Positive feedback enhances contractions, not inhibits them.

scaffolds.q: How does positive feedback differ from negative feedback in terms of the response?
options: Positive ENHANCES and ACCELERATES; negative OPPOSES and STABILIZES
scaffolds.explain: POSITIVE feedback ENHANCES and ACCELERATES the response (drives toward completion). NEGATIVE feedback OPPOSES changes and STABILIZES (maintains homeostasis).

wrongExplains.0: They have opposite effects on body processes.
wrongExplains.2: Positive feedback accelerates; it's not slower.
wrongExplains.3: Negative feedback maintains homeostasis; positive drives completion.

options: Because it amplifies — adds to — the original stimulus
scaffolds.explain: 'POSITIVE' means the feedback ADDS TO (amplifies) the original stimulus. The name refers to direction, not whether it's good or bad.

wrongExplains.0: The name refers to amplification, not health benefit.
wrongExplains.2: The name refers to direction of effect, not electrical charge.
wrongExplains.3: The name refers to amplification; positive feedback can be dangerous.

scaffolds.q: Is this TRUE: Positive feedback mechanisms enhance the original stimulus so that the response is accelerated?
options: True — this is the defining characteristic of positive feedback
False — positive feedback has no effect on stimulus
scaffolds.explain: TRUE. This is the DEFINING CHARACTERISTIC of positive feedback — it ENHANCES the stimulus and ACCELERATES the response until completion.

wrongExplains.0: Opposing changes is negative feedback, not positive.
wrongExplains.2: Positive feedback powerfully amplifies the stimulus.
wrongExplains.3: Positive feedback accelerates, not slows, responses.
